roc_curves.title: How to Read ROC Curves
roc_curves.body_markdown:
ROC curves show how well the model separates classes across decision thresholds.

**False Positive Rate**
The fraction of negative samples that are incorrectly predicted as positive.

**True Positive Rate**
The fraction of positive samples that are correctly identified.

**Class-specific ROC curves**
Each line shows a one-vs-rest ROC curve for one class. These plots make it easier to see which classes are easier or harder for the model to distinguish.

**AUC in the legend**
Area Under the Curve. Higher values generally indicate better class separation, with `1.0` being ideal and `0.5` being close to random guessing.

**Threshold-colored markers**
On the class-specific plots, marker color reflects the probability threshold along the ROC curve. This helps connect operating points to stricter or looser decision thresholds.

**Macro-average ROC**
An unweighted average of the class-wise ROC curves. This treats all classes equally and is useful when class balance matters.

**Micro-average ROC**
A global summary across all one-vs-rest decisions. This is useful when you want an overall view weighted by sample counts.
